context.story_engine: Noble houses compete for power across multiple kingdoms, testing how far they'll go for survival and control

plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth about Jon Arryn's death and protect the realm
plotlines.obstacle: court intrigue, Lannister power, his own moral inflexibility
plotlines.stakes: his life, his family's safety, the stability of the realm

plotlines.name: Cersei: Power
plotlines.goal: protect her children's claim to the throne and maintain Lannister control
plotlines.obstacle: Ned's investigation, Robert's potential discovery of the truth
plotlines.stakes: her children's lives, her own execution

plotlines.name: Jon: Belonging
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose at the Night's Watch
plotlines.obstacle: his privileged background, the reality of the Watch, divided loyalties
plotlines.stakes: his identity, his honor, his duty

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Transformation
plotlines.goal: evolve from powerless exile to true leader and reclaim her birthright
plotlines.obstacle: Dothraki culture, her brother's control, assassination attempts
plotlines.stakes: her life, her unborn child, the Targaryen legacy

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive false accusations and navigate family conflicts
plotlines.obstacle: Catelyn's arrest, Lysa's madness, his family's schemes

plotlines.name: Catelyn: Protection
plotlines.goal: protect her family from Lannister threats
plotlines.obstacle: incomplete information, political complexity, distance from her children
plotlines.stakes: her children's lives, her family's destruction

plotlines.name: Arya: Independence
plotlines.goal: maintain her identity and freedom in a dangerous world
plotlines.obstacle: court expectations, Lannister pursuit, her father's fall
plotlines.stakes: her life, her sense of self

plotlines.name: Robb: War
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and defend the North
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister military power, political alliances, inexperience

plotlines.name: Night's Watch: Supernatural Threat
plotlines.goal: investigate and defend against the supernatural threat beyond the Wall
plotlines.obstacle: unknown enemy, institutional skepticism, limited resources
plotlines.stakes: the realm's survival, the Watch's purpose

events.event: Three Night's Watch rangers discover wildlings slaughtered in ritual pattern
White Walker kills two rangers, Will flees south
Ned executes deserter Will for abandoning his post
Stark children find dead direwolf with pups, each claims one
Jon claims the albino runt and names it Ghost
Raven brings news of Jon Arryn's death
King Robert arrives at Winterfell with the Lannisters
Robert visits Lyanna's crypt and asks Ned to be Hand
Robert proposes marriage between Joffrey and Sansa
Catelyn urges Ned to be cautious about Robert's offer
Viserys arranges Daenerys's marriage to Khal Drogo for an army
Daenerys submits to the marriage arrangement despite her terror
Jorah Mormont presents himself and offers Daenerys books
Catelyn receives Lysa's letter accusing Lannisters of murder
Ned decides he must go south to investigate Jon Arryn's death
Bran discovers Cersei and Jaime in sexual embrace
Jaime pushes Bran from the window to protect their secret

episodes.theme: the price of secrets and honor

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore how characters respond to impossible choices that define their identity
Lysa's letter and Bran's discovery both force Ned into the dangerous game of thrones
the audience knows the Lannisters' secret that Ned is unknowingly walking into

patches.reason: White Walker events don't fit existing plotlines - this appears to be a separate supernatural threat storyline

events.event: Maester Luwin tells Catelyn and Robb that Bran will never walk again if he lives
Catelyn refuses to leave Bran's bedside, neglecting Winterfell's affairs
Jaime expresses unease to Cersei about what Bran might remember
Cersei dismisses the risk, confident a child's word means nothing against theirs
Jon decides to join the Night's Watch and travels north with Tyrion
Jon gives Arya a small sword she names Needle before leaving
Jon says farewell to the unconscious Bran
Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya
Sansa and Joffrey walk together along the Trident river
They encounter Arya sparring with the butcher's boy Mycah
Joffrey threatens Mycah with his sword
Arya's direwolf Nymeria bites Joffrey's arm in defense
Arya drives Nymeria away to save the wolf from punishment
Robert holds court on the matter and Cersei demands a direwolf be killed
Robert orders Sansa's innocent direwolf Lady executed instead
Ned carries out the sentence himself
The Hound hunts down and kills the butcher's boy
Daenerys struggles with painful nights with Drogo and grueling days of riding
Jorah rides alongside Daenerys and begins explaining Dothraki customs
Daenerys learns from her handmaiden how to assert herself with Drogo physically
Daenerys faces Drogo as an equal rather than a victim, shifting their marriage dynamic
An assassin enters Bran's room with a Valyrian steel dagger to kill him
Catelyn fights the assassin bare-handed until Bran's direwolf kills him
The murder attempt convinces Catelyn the Lannisters are trying to silence Bran
Catelyn resolves to ride south secretly to warn Ned

interactions.description: all three plotlines show characters losing innocence through violence—Arya through Lady's death, Daenerys through sexual agency, Catelyn through attempted murder
the audience knows the Lannisters are behind Bran's fall, while Catelyn only suspects after the assassination attempt
Ned's honor forces him to execute Lady, directly harming Arya's world and trust

events.event: Jon arrives at Castle Black and discovers most recruits are criminals and outcasts
Jon dominates in the practice yard due to his superior training
Jon's dominance earns him resentment from other recruits
Tyrion advises Jon to stop acting like a privileged lord's son
Jon begins helping the weaker boys train
Ned arrives in King's Landing and takes his seat on the Small Council
Ned meets Littlefinger, Varys, and Grand Maester Pycelle
Ned learns the Crown owes millions to the Lannisters and Iron Bank
Robert demands a lavish tournament despite Ned's objections
Catelyn arrives in King's Landing in secret
Littlefinger arranges a private meeting between Catelyn and Ned
Catelyn shows Ned the Valyrian steel dagger from Bran's assassination attempt
Littlefinger identifies the dagger as one he lost to Tyrion in a bet
Ned and Catelyn agree she must return north while he investigates
Arya chases cats through the Red Keep and stumbles into the cellar
Arya overhears two men discussing Stark-Lannister tension and war plans
Arya fails to convince her father of the conversation's significance
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt so she can explore on foot
Viserys strikes Daenerys for defying his authority
Dothraki guards intervene and Daenerys allows them to humiliate Viserys
Viserys realizes his sister is no longer under his control

episodes.theme: the cost of privilege and the necessity of adaptation

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters learning to navigate new environments and shed old assumptions
Ned and Catelyn's investigation merges as they share information about the Lannister threat
Arya overhears crucial information about the conspiracy that Ned doesn't know, while the audience sees the larger web of intrigue

events.event: Ned visits the blacksmith's apprentice Gendry, who bears a striking resemblance to young Robert Baratheon
Ned realizes Robert has bastard children throughout King's Landing, all with black hair
Ned obtains the genealogical history book Jon Arryn was studying before his death
Tyrion departs Castle Black heading south
Tyrion stops at Winterfell and gives Bran designs for a special saddle
Robb receives Tyrion coldly, suspicious of all Lannisters
Daenerys discovers she is pregnant with Drogo's child
Jorah tells Daenerys privately that a Targaryen heir changes the political calculation in Westeros
Daenerys announces her pregnancy to Drogo, who declares the child will be the Stallion Who Mounts the World
Viserys grows increasingly bitter that the Dothraki show him no deference
The tournament of the Hand begins at King's Landing
Ser Gregor Clegane kills Ser Hugh of the Vale during a joust
Littlefinger tells Sansa the story of Gregor and the Hound
Ned suspects Ser Hugh's death was not accidental and that someone is eliminating witnesses
Catelyn recognizes Tyrion among travelers at the Crossroads Inn
Catelyn calls on her father's bannermen and arrests Tyrion for attempting to murder Bran
Catelyn takes Tyrion east toward the Eyrie to avoid Lannister pursuit

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters discovering dangerous truths that will change everything
Catelyn's arrest of Tyrion directly threatens his survival while serving her protective goals
the audience understands that Cersei's faction is eliminating witnesses while Ned slowly pieces together the truth

events.event: Robert receives word that Daenerys is pregnant and orders her assassination
Ned argues fiercely against murdering Daenerys and her unborn child
Robert refuses to relent on the assassination order
Ned resigns as Hand of the King in protest
Ned announces he will return to Winterfell
Varys warns Ned that his investigation has made him a target
Varys reveals that Jon Arryn was poisoned
Catelyn's party is ambushed by hill tribes on the high road
Tyrion fights alongside his captors to survive the ambush
Catelyn's party reaches the Eyrie with Tyrion
Lysa Arryn imprisons Tyrion in the sky cells
Tyrion discovers Lysa has already convicted him in her mind
Jon and his fellow recruits prepare to take their vows at Castle Black
Jon is assigned to the stewards rather than the rangers
Sam explains that steward assignment is preparation for command, not punishment
Arya practices sword training with Syrio Forel
Ned watches Arya's lesson and his expression darkens hearing wooden swords clash
Littlefinger takes Ned to a brothel to see another of Robert's bastards
Ned realizes all Baratheon children have black hair while Cersei's are golden-haired
Ned discovers Joffrey, Myrcella, and Tommen are products of Cersei and Jaime's incest
Jaime learns of Tyrion's arrest
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets with armed men
Jaime's soldiers kill Ned's guards in the street fight
Jaime personally wounds Ned before riding away demanding Tyrion's release

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a corrupt world

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore doing what's right versus what's practical for survival
Ned's discovery of the incest secret directly threatens Cersei's power, leading to Jaime's violent response
the audience knows Robert's assassination order against Daenerys will fail to stop her rise to power

events.event: Ned wakes from his injuries to find Robert has returned from hunting
Robert threatens to give the Hand position to Jaime if Ned refuses to resume duties
Ned reluctantly agrees to resume his duties as Hand
Robert rides out hunting again, leaving Ned to sit the Iron Throne
Villagers petition the court about Ser Gregor Clegane's raids in the Riverlands
Ned recognizes the raids as Lannister provocation to draw the Tullys into conflict
Ned sends Lord Beric Dondarrion to bring Gregor to justice
Ned summons Tywin Lannister to answer for his bannerman's crimes
Tyrion demands a trial by combat rather than face Lysa's biased judgment
Tyrion recruits the sellsword Bronn to fight as his champion
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen using speed and cunning against armored strength
Lysa is forced to release Tyrion, who walks out free with Bronn
Viserys arrives drunk at a Dothraki feast and draws his sword in Vaes Dothrak
Viserys threatens Daenerys and demands the crown Drogo promised him
Drogo melts gold and pours it over Viserys's head, killing him
Daenerys watches her brother die without flinching and declares he was no dragon
Jorah observes Daenerys's transformation with admiration and concern
Jon Snow and Sam take their vows before a heart tree beyond the Wall
Jon's direwolf Ghost emerges carrying a severed human hand
Ned studies the genealogy book and confirms all Baratheons have black-haired children
Ned holds the secret that could destroy the Lannisters but wrestles with how to use it

episodes.theme: the price of truth and justice

interactions.description: all plotlines explore characters facing defining moments that test their core values and identity
the audience knows Ned has discovered the truth about Joffrey's parentage while the Lannisters remain unaware
Ned's investigation directly threatens Cersei's position, setting up inevitable conflict

events.event: Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children are Jaime's
Ned offers Cersei the chance to flee with her children before Robert returns
Cersei tells Ned that in the game of thrones, you win or you die
Robert is brought back mortally wounded by a boar during the hunt
Robert dictates his will to Ned, naming him Protector of the Realm
Ned writes 'rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in Robert's will
Robert dies
Ned sends a letter to Stannis informing him of the truth about Cersei's children
Ned asks Littlefinger to secure the City Watch's loyalty
Littlefinger advises Ned to make peace with the Lannisters and rule through Joffrey
Ned refuses to compromise with what he sees as a usurper
Renly offers Ned backing of his household guard to seize Joffrey
Ned refuses Renly's offer, unwilling to frighten a child with soldiers
Renly flees King's Landing
A wine merchant tries to poison Daenerys
Jorah Mormont intervenes and exposes the assassination plot
The assassination attempt enrages Drogo, who vows to conquer Westeros
Bodies of two dead rangers are brought inside Castle Black
The corpses reanimate as wights in the night
Jon fights a wight that attacks the Lord Commander
Jon saves the Lord Commander by setting the wight on fire

episodes.theme: the price of honor in a world without it

interactions.description: all plotlines explore the tension between moral principles and survival—Ned's honor vs political reality, Cersei's maternal protection vs ruthless power, Daenerys finding strength through adversity, Jon proving himself through duty
Robert's assassination attempt on Daenerys ironically triggers the very invasion he feared, while Ned's mercy toward Cersei enables his own downfall
Ned and Cersei's confrontation directly pits honor against power, with Robert's death forcing the conflict into the open

events.event: Ned enters the throne room to present Robert's will naming him Protector of the Realm
Cersei tears up the will and declares Joffrey king
On Littlefinger's signal, the gold cloaks turn on Ned's men and kill his household guard
Littlefinger puts a dagger to Ned's throat and tells him he warned not to trust him
Ned is arrested and imprisoned in the dungeons beneath the Red Keep
Sansa is kept in her chambers under Lannister guard, unaware of the full betrayal
Syrio Forel fights Lannister soldiers with a wooden practice sword to help Arya escape
Syrio is overwhelmed and presumably killed by the Lannister soldiers
Arya recovers Needle and hides in the streets of King's Landing
Drogo's khalasar raids a village to fund the invasion of Westeros
Daenerys orders the Dothraki to stop raping captive women and takes them under her protection
Mago challenges Daenerys's order, angering Drogo's warriors
Drogo fights and kills Mago in single combat despite taking a chest wound
Mirri Maz Duur offers to treat Drogo's wound and Daenerys accepts her help
Lord Commander Mormont rewards Jon with Longclaw for saving his life from the wight
Jon receives word that Ned has been arrested for treason and attempts to desert
Sam, Grenn, and Pyp convince Jon to honor his vow and return to the Watch
Tyrion recruits the hill tribes by promising Tywin's gold and weapons
Tyrion arrives at Tywin's war camp where an army is assembling against the Riverlands

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a world without it

interactions.description: all three heroes face moments where their principles are tested against survival
Ned and Cersei's conflict reaches its climax in the throne room confrontation
Jon doesn't know the full extent of his father's downfall when he tries to desert

events.event: Robb Stark calls the northern bannermen to war and marches south with twenty thousand men to rescue his father
Catelyn negotiates passage at the Twins by promising Robb will marry one of Frey's daughters and Arya will marry one of Frey's sons
Robb splits his forces, sending a feint toward Tywin's army while leading the bulk to Riverrun
The feint engages Tywin's forces in the Battle of the Green Fork, where Tyrion fights and is knocked unconscious
Tywin wins the battle but realizes too late that the main Stark army was not there
Robb's force ambushes Jaime's army at the Battle of the Whispering Wood and wins decisively
Robb personally captures Jaime Lannister
Catelyn confronts the captive Jaime, who taunts her but cannot deny his vulnerability
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon and urges him to confess to treason for Sansa's sake
Ned agrees to lie for his daughter's sake, abandoning his principles
Ned publicly confesses that he conspired against the rightful king at the Great Sept
Joffrey orders Ned's execution despite Cersei and the councilors expecting mercy
Ser Ilyn Payne beheads Ned Stark with his own sword Ice
Sansa screams as her father is executed
Yoren grabs Arya from the crowd, shields her eyes and pulls her away
Drogo's wound festers into a deadly infection
Daenerys desperately asks Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo
Mirri agrees but warns that only death pays for life
Daenerys goes into premature labor and is carried into the tent during the dark magic ritual

episodes.theme: the price of power and honor

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore the cost of maintaining principles versus survival—Ned dies for his honor, Cersei loses control despite her schemes, Daenerys risks everything for love
Ned's execution is the climax of both his honor arc and Cersei's power struggle, with Joffrey's decision affecting both
Robb wins his battles to save his father just as Ned is being executed, unknown to him

events.event: Daenerys wakes to find Drogo alive but in a vegetative state
Daenerys discovers her son was stillborn and monstrous
Mirri reveals she acted deliberately as revenge for her village's destruction
Most of the khalasar abandons Daenerys for a new khal
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at Ned's head on a spike
Sansa nearly pushes Joffrey from the rampart but the Hound stops her
Arya travels north disguised as Arry with Yoren and Night's Watch recruits
Gendry joins the group, unknowingly fleeing Lannister hunters
Northern lords debate their next move at the Twins
Greatjon Umber declares Robb the King in the North
The northern lords kneel and proclaim Robb king
Catelyn strikes the captive Jaime in grief and anger
Lord Commander Mormont tells Jon his duty lies with the Watch
Mormont announces the great ranging to find Benjen and investigate White Walkers
Jon rides out with the Night's Watch into the haunted forest
Daenerys builds a funeral pyre for Drogo and places her dragon eggs on it
Daenerys ties Mirri to the flames as a final sacrifice
Daenerys walks into the fire over Jorah's protests
Daenerys emerges unburned with three living dragons
Her remaining followers kneel before Daenerys

interactions.description: all plotlines involve characters being reborn into new roles—Daenerys as dragon queen, Robb as king, Jon as true brother of the Watch, Arya as a survivor
Ned's execution serves both as the final consequence of his honor and Cersei's ultimate victory in protecting her power
the audience knows Gendry is Robert's bastard and therefore a target, but he travels unknowingly
